privileged_info_summary: The task requires comparing the safety of Tizanidine and Baclofen when co-administered with Rucaparib. Medical literature and drug interaction databases confirm that Rucaparib is a moderate inhibitor of CYP1A2, while Tizanidine is a CYP1A2 substrate. This creates a severe interaction risk (typically red or orange on interaction charts) due to elevated Tizanidine levels. Baclofen is not significantly metabolized by CYP enzymes and is renally excreted, meaning it does not have this pharmacokinetic interaction with Rucaparib (typically green or grey on charts). Therefore, Baclofen is medically verified as the safer muscle relaxant choice. Although the task.json file was truncated and missing its metadata section, the hardcoded evaluation criteria in verifier.py strictly and correctly match these real-world pharmacological facts.
